title: simarouba laevis
intro: Simarouba laevis, also known as the "Quina de São Francisco", is a plant species belonging to the Simaroubaceae family and is native to the tropical regions of South America. The medicinal properties of Simarouba laevis include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to help manage fever, reduce inflammation, and treat gastrointestinal issues, as well as its potential to combat certain types of cancer and infections. Simarouba laevis can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its bark, leaves, and roots are commonly used in traditional medicine in various forms of preparations.
